name: Villamayor de Calatrava from Puertollano — circular via Almodóvar del Campo
name_original: Villamayor de Calatrava desde Puertollano — circular por Almodóvar del Campo
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Puertollano traverse a landscape shaped by volcanic terrain and industrial heritage, situated in Spain's Castilla-La Mancha region. The area features maars and Strombolian volcanic cones, notably within the Campo de Calatrava, offering varied geological sights. Routes often lead through the expansive Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park, providing scenic views and diverse ecosystems. The Vía Verde del Carbón y la Plata, an old railway line, allows cyclists to explore mining landscapes and natural beauty. Best touring cycling routes around Puertollano The most popular touring cycling route is Montoro and Tablillas Reservoirs Loop, a 27.4 miles (44.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain around the reservoirs. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Climb to the Castle Convent of Calatrava La Nueva — circular from Puertollano, a difficult 44.9 miles (72.2 km) path. This route offers cultural exploration of the castle and views across the Valle de Alcudia. Local touring cyclists also love the Hinojosas de Calatrava and Almodóvar del Campo loop, a 36.8 miles (59.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Puertollano is defined by volcanic terrain, industrial heritage, and natural parks like the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Puertollano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Puertollano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Puertollano?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Puertollano, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include options for various fitness levels, from easy rides to more challenging adventures.

Q: What makes touring cycling in Puertollano unique?
A: Touring cycling in Puertollano is unique due to its fascinating blend of volcanic landscapes, rich industrial heritage, and expansive natural parks. You'll encounter distinctive geological sights like maars and Strombolian volcanic cones in the Campo de Calatrava, alongside the scenic beauty of the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Puertollano?
A: The touring cycling routes in Puertollano are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the blend of natural beauty and historical sites, and the opportunities for both challenging climbs and scenic, relaxed rides.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Puertollano?
A: Yes, Puertollano offers 6 easy touring cycling routes, which are generally suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable experience for all skill levels.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes around Puertollano?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 27 difficult routes around Puertollano. These often feature significant elevation gains and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058415241/montoro-and-tablillas-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montoro and Tablillas Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 27.4 miles with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Puertollano?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Puertollano are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052875068/climb-to-the-castle-convent-of-calatrava-la-nueva-circular-from-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Castle Convent of Calatrava La Nueva — circular from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052674274/villamayor-de-calatrava-from-puertollano-circular-via-almodovar-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamayor de Calatrava from Puertollano — circular via Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate experience.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes will I encounter on touring cycling routes near Puertollano?
A: You'll encounter diverse natural landscapes, from the unique volcanic terrain of the Campo de Calatrava, featuring maars like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, to the expansive forests and panoramic views of the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park. The Sierra de Calatrava also provides a picturesque mountainous backdrop.

Q: Are there historical or cultural attractions I can visit while cycling in Puertollano?
A: Yes, Puertollano's industrial past and historical significance offer several attractions. You can cycle along the Vía Verde del Carbón y la Plata, an old railway line showcasing mining heritage, or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Calatrava la Nueva and Sacred Convent&lt;/a&gt;. Within Puertollano, the Mining Museum and the Monument to the Miner on Cerro de Santa Ana provide historical context, with the latter also offering panoramic city views.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural monuments to discover?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer scenic viewpoints, especially those traversing the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park. You can also visit the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7199638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Agria&lt;/a&gt;, known for its mineral-rich water, or enjoy views from the Cerro de Santa Ana. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is another unique natural sight.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in Puertollano?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058415241/montoro-and-tablillas-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montoro and Tablillas Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 10 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374615022/hinojosas-de-calatrava-and-almod" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hinojosas de Calatrava and Almodóvar del Campo loop&lt;/a&gt; averages about 3 hours 44 minutes. Shorter, easier routes will take less time, while longer, more challenging ones can extend to several hours.

Q: Are there routes that explore the industrial heritage of Puertollano?
A: Yes, the region's industrial past is a significant feature. The Vía Verde del Carbón y la Plata (Greenway of Coal and Silver) is a prime example, allowing cyclists to ride along an old railway line through mining landscapes of the Ojailén valley and the Alcudia valley, blending industrial history with natural beauty.

Q: What kind of surfaces can I expect on touring cycling routes in Puertollano?
A: You can expect a mix of surfaces. Many routes feature mostly paved roads, ideal for touring bikes. However, some segments, particularly within natural parks or on Vías Verdes, may include unpaved sections, offering a diverse cycling experience. It's advisable to check individual route details for specific surface information.
tips:
The Almodóvar del Campo Lagoon is a unique natural and geological feature located in the province of Ciudad Real, within the Campo de Calatrava region, famous for its volcanic landscapes.

It is a lagoon formed in the crater of a volcano after a phreatomagmatic eruption (interaction between magma and groundwater). This type of lagoon is called a maar.

In the Alcudia Valley, we find the municipality of Almodóvar del Campo built on the side of a volcano, so present in Campo de Calatrava, and whose crater is now a lagoon. The Arab footprint is the most emblematic of the city, leaving behind the memory of the Castle and above all the unique Parish of Our Lady of the Assumption. There you can see the first videomapping in Spain permanently installed in a closed space, under the theme of the life and work of San Juan de Ávila.
Section of Highway CM-4115 through the Dehesa Labrada, good track and beautiful landscape.
